repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

comment_data.body:
Not sure we need a new function. I was expecting instead to add a callback arg to `get_configs` https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/36775#issuecomment-4106304954

cc @superatomic

`get_configs()` would probably then need a filter for config's name. To use it like:

```lua
vim.lsp.get_configs({ name = "lua_ls" }, function(config)
  vim.lsp.start(config, ...)
end)
```

`get_configs()` returns `vim.lsp.Config`s, and I'm pretty sure the point of `resolve_config()` is to turn  `vim.lsp.Config`s into `vim.lsp.ClientConfig`s (so they can be passed into `vim.lsp.start()`). I unfortunately don't think we can reuse `get_configs()` here.
This may be a little bit of a stretch, but we could still return `vim.lsp.Config` directly from `get_configs()` and provide a `vim.lsp.ClientConfig` as the callback argument.

Not worth it, I think. Especially because `resolve_config()` needs a `bufnr` argument to resolve a config (should be renamed to `buf`, btw!) which `get_configs()` does not need. And a `{ name = ... }` filter to `get_configs()` is silly; if you have a name, you should just be using `vim.lsp.config[name]`.
If it's part of the api, it's a bit sad one cant retreive the error, aka `return nil, "workspace_required=true, no workspace found"`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -459,6 +459,47 @@ function lsp.get_configs(filter)
   return configs
 end
 
+--- Resolves a |vim.lsp.Config| into a fully usable |vim.lsp.ClientConfig|.
+--- Aborts if the config requires a workspace and none can be found.
+---
+--- @param bufnr integer Buffer number the config is being resolved for.
+--- @param config vim.lsp.Config Base LSP configuration to resolve.
+--- @param on_resolve fun(config: vim.lsp.ClientConfig, opts: vim.lsp.start.Opts) Called with the resolved config.
+--- @return vim.lsp.ClientConfig?
+function lsp.resolve_config(bufnr, config, on_resolve)
+  if not config.root_dir and config.root_markers then
+    config = vim.deepcopy(config)
+    config.root_dir = vim.fs.root(bufnr, config.root_markers)
+  end
+
+  if
+    not config.root_dir
+    and (not config.workspace_folders or #config.workspace_folders == 0)
+    and config.workspace_required
+  then
+    log.info(
+      ('skipping config "%s": workspace_required=true, no workspace found'):format(config.name)
+    )

comment_data.body: I would move `and config.workspace_required` before the ` and (not config.workspace_folders or #config.workspace_folders == 0)` since the condition is easier to check so it can branch out earlier ?

comment_data.body: Even as someone familiar with LSP, I find this description confusing. What does "resolve" mean in this context? An example of a popular well-behaved LSP server that requires this would be helpful.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1319,6 +1319,22 @@ omnifunc({findstart}, {base})                             *vim.lsp.omnifunc()*
       • |complete-items|
       • |CompleteDone|
 
+                                                    *vim.lsp.resolve_config()*
+resolve_config({bufnr}, {config}, {on_resolve})
+    Resolves a |vim.lsp.Config| into a fully usable |vim.lsp.ClientConfig|.

comment_data.body: I would make these optional with reasonable defaults to match other LSP methods: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/74cc0364f1706ae8df84f1750251b6a0dd3e43fd/runtime/doc/lsp.txt#L2099-L2114

comment_data.body: Why don't we need to set `noref=true` here? What's the justification of this usage of `deepcopy` not being able to cause a cyclic reference?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -459,6 +459,47 @@ function lsp.get_configs(filter)
   return configs
 end
 
+--- Resolves a |vim.lsp.Config| into a fully usable |vim.lsp.ClientConfig|.
+--- Aborts if the config requires a workspace and none can be found.
+---
+--- @param bufnr integer Buffer number the config is being resolved for.
+--- @param config vim.lsp.Config Base LSP configuration to resolve.
+--- @param on_resolve fun(config: vim.lsp.ClientConfig, opts: vim.lsp.start.Opts) Called with the resolved config.
+--- @return vim.lsp.ClientConfig?
+function lsp.resolve_config(bufnr, config, on_resolve)
+  if not config.root_dir and config.root_markers then
+    config = vim.deepcopy(config)
+    config.root_dir = vim.fs.root(bufnr, config.root_markers)
+  end
+
+  if
+    not config.root_dir
+    and (not config.workspace_folders or #config.workspace_folders == 0)
+    and config.workspace_required
+  then
+    log.info(
+      ('skipping config "%s": workspace_required=true, no workspace found'):format(config.name)
+    )
+    return nil
+  end
+
+  -- Deepcopy config so changes done in the client
+  -- do not propagate back to the enabled configs.
+  config = vim.deepcopy(config)
